response.justification: Wei Chen is a versatile polyglot developer with genuine depth in web, distributed systems, and developer tooling, but is disqualified from this role on the primary criterion alone: the minimum requires 8 years of C++ experience, and the candidate's entire C++ exposure amounts to roughly 6 months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 plus a bachelor's thesis project — C++ does not even appear in the skills section. Beyond the C++ gap, the role demands 5+ years of large-scale infrastructure and cloud compute platform ownership; the candidate's infrastructure work is limited to Kubernetes deployments for an open-source side project and AWS CDK usage in a SaaS pricing team. There is no record of formal technical leadership over cloud or systems engineering teams.
strengths: Demonstrated experience with high-throughput distributed systems at Dunnhumby, handling millions of HTTP requests per second using low-latency async I/O patterns
Broad DevOps and infrastructure-adjacent exposure: Kubernetes, Docker, CI/CD pipelines, GitLab administration, and AWS CDK
Active knowledge-sharing record through workshops, conference talks, and open-source libraries, indicating mentoring aptitude
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months total) — critically short of the 8-year hard minimum; C++ is absent from the listed skills entirely
No evidence of owning or leading a cloud compute or large-scale infrastructure platform at any point in the career history
No formal technical leadership role; career is entirely as an individual contributor, with no record of driving cross-team strategy, roadmaps, or architectural standards at the team lead level
